metas.name: Futurama - S1E1
metas.description: On New Year's Eve 1999, Pizza Delivery boy Philip J. Fry accidentally falls into a cryogenic chamber and is frozen for 1,000 years. Finally unfrozen, he explores New New York, meets his new best friend (a kleptomaniacal robot named Bender) and goes to work for his great-great-great-great-grand-nephew's space delivery business.

metas.name: Futurama - S1E2
metas.description: For the crew's first mission, they must deliver a crate full of toys for an arcade game to the moon. Fry is very excited, while it is not really a big deal in the year 3000. Fry tries to persuade Leela to go exploring on the moon, while Amy tries to recover the keys to the Planet Express Ship.

metas.name: Futurama - S1E3
metas.description: When it is discovered that Philip has been living in the Planet Express office, he is forced to move out. Trying to sleep standing up in Bender's closet-sized apartment doesn't work, so they go looking for a place both can be comfortable. Unfortunately, Bender is incompatible with the ideal apartment they do find until he has a radical... modification which makes him less than happy.

metas.name: Futurama - S1E4
metas.description: On a mission intended to save endangered animals on a collapsing planet, Leela and the crew run into legendary starship captain Zapp Brannigan. A self-proclaimed ladies man, Zapp sees Leela as a potential new conquest. When the captain refuses to aid the animal rescue, Leela and her crew try to leave Zapp's starship. But Zapp throws Fry and Bender in jail, and summons Leela to his "Lovenasium". They ultimately escape and arrive on the doomed planet, where Leela finally finds love - with a cute, and very useful, creature named Nibbler.

metas.name: Futurama - S1E5
metas.description: Fry and Leela disguise themselves as robots to find Bender after he fails to return from a delivery to a robot planet, only to learn that he has become a celebrity in a culture which fears humans.

metas.name: Futurama - S1E6
metas.description: Fry discovers his bank account interest has made him a billionaire and buys the one thing the year 3000 doesn't have, anchovies.

metas.name: Futurama - S1E7
metas.description: Delivery Boy Philip J. Fry is promoted to Emperor after accidentally drinking his fluid-based predecessor.

metas.name: Futurama - S1E8
metas.description: A big piece of garbage that was released a thousand years ago is now on the way to Earth to destroy it. After Fry, Leela and Bender fail on placing a bomb on it, the city has to build another big piece of garbage, yet that may be a problem since trash doesn't exist in year 3000.

metas.name: Futurama - S1E9
metas.description: During a Beastie Boys concert, Bender runs into a high school buddy, Fender. He invites him to a party, which turns out to be robots getting high off of electricity, or "jacking on". After one try, Bender is hooked.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E1
metas.description: The Planet Express crew take a space cruise vacation. Leela tells Captain Zapp Brannigan that Fry is her boyfriend and Amy tells the same thing to her parents. Bender falls in love with a rich female robot. Problems start when the spaceship approaches a black hole.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E2
metas.description: Fry, Leela, Bender and Farnsworth have to go to Mars University where Fry has to share a room with a talking monkey, Farnsworth's last invention. The monkey becomes a real headache when he starts beating Fry in all areas. Meanwhile, Bender is hanging out with nerd robots.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E3
metas.description: The Omicronians invade Earth, demanding to see the lost final episode of the 1999 TV show Single Female Lawyer. The Planet Express crew must re-enact the episode to appease the invading aliens.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E4
metas.description: When Fry wins a free tour of the Slurm soda factory, he and his friends split off from the tour group and make a horrifying discovery concerning the "secret ingredient" that makes Slurm so addictive.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E5
metas.description: Professor Farnsworth installs an empathy chip in Bender after the inconsiderate robot flushes Nibbler down the toilet. With his newfound emotions, a concerned Bender ventures into the mutant-infested sewers to rescue Leela's beloved pet.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E6
metas.description: Upon destroying the new DOOP headquarters, Zapp Brannigan and Kif find themselves court-martialed and dishonorably discharged. They take up jobs at Planet Express, where Kif learns what respect feels like and Zapp incites a mutiny against Leela.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E7
metas.description: When the price of titanium skyrockets, Bender pawns his titanium-rich body and lives the high life as just a head. But when the head-in-a-jar of Richard M. Nixon uses Bender's body to run for election for President of Earth, Bender vows to get his rightful property back.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E8
metas.description: To apologize for an inconsiderate remark, Fry searches for the perfect Xmas gift for Leela, all the while trying to avoid the murderous SantaBot, whose standards for niceness are astronomically high.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E9
metas.description: When Dr. Zoidberg runs amok during the Decapodian mating season, Fry sets out to teach him how to woo a mate. Things backfire when Zoidberg's desired mate falls for Fry instead, and Zoidberg challenges Fry to a battle to the death.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E10
metas.description: Fry's brief relationship with Amy is forcibly prolonged when a near-fatal car crash leaves Fry's head grafted to Amy's left shoulder. Meanwhile, Bender cashes in on Valentine's Day by starting a dating service.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E11
metas.description: Fry suspects Bender's new buddy, a bending unit named Flexo who looks almost exactly like Bender, of being pure evil. When a valuable atom of Jumbonium goes missing, Fry suspects Flexo, but can't tell which robot is which.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E12
metas.description: Bender is the featured attraction in the Ultimate Robot Fighting League, but his glory is short-lived when he is instructed to intentionally lose his next match. Leela vows to lead Bender to victory, in an effort to show up her unsupportive martial arts teacher.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E13
metas.description: Leela meets Alkazar, who claims to be the only other remaining cyclops in the universe. But when Alkazar invites Leela back to his home planet and treats her like a slave, Fry suspects that something is amiss.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E14
metas.description: When Hermes takes a stress-relieving vacation, replacement bureaucrat Morgan Proctor becomes infatuated with Fry. Bender threatens to publicize their affair, but Morgan removes Bender's memory and hides it within the cavernous Central Bureaucracy.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E15
metas.description: Professor Farnsworth unveils his clone, Cubert Farnsworth, whom he plans to leave everything to upon his retirement. But Cubert wants no part of Farnsworth's lifestyle, prompting the professor into a premature retirement on the Near-Death Star.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E16
metas.description: A fishing trip over the ocean takes a turn for the worse when a colossal mouth bass pulls the Planet Express ship to the bottom of the sea. There, Fry falls in love with a mermaid named Umbriel, and the crew discovers the sunken city of Atlanta, Georgia.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E17
metas.description: Bender takes a new job in the Robot Mafia, but his loyalty is tested when he goes along on the robotic gangster's efforts to rob the Planet Express ship.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E18
metas.description: The crew discovers an irresistible source of food on a distant planet, and brings it back to Earth to be sold at the Fishy Joe's restaurant chain. But when it's discovered that the so-called "Popplers" are actually Omicronian babies, the Omicronians demand recompense.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E19
metas.description: Mom reprograms the world's robots to rebel against humanity. The only hope of salvation is Mom's old flame - Professor Farnsworth, who must rekindle his romance with Mom in order to save mankind.

metas.name: Futurama - S2E20
metas.description: The Professor's "What-If" machine simulates Bender, Leela and Fry's wishes. Bender discovers what it would be like if he were 500 feet tall, Leela discovers what it would be like if she were more impulsive, and Fry discovers what would happen if he never came to the future.

metas.name: Futurama - S3E1
metas.description: When the inheritance Bender claims in the Old Country isn't quite what he expected, the Planet Express crew has to find the source of an age-old curse.

metas.name: Futurama - S3E2
metas.description: Fry and Bender enlist in the army to get a soldiers discount for bubblegum. When they are called to fight a war on a planet of bouncing ball aliens, Leela disguises herself as man to enlist with them. Bender is eventually sent to parley with the enemy, but this turns out to be a ploy.

metas.name: Futurama - S3E3
metas.description: Fry is reunited with his old girlfriend from the 20th Century, but she doesn't adapt to the 31st century as well as he has. Convincing Fry to return to the cryogenic chamber, they awaken in a barren wasteland and struggle to make a place for themselves.

metas.name: Futurama - S3E4
metas.description: When Fry becomes infested with parasitic worms that make him stronger and smarter, he finally finds the perfect way to profess his feelings to Leela. Meanwhile, the rest of the crew goes on a journey into Fry's body to eradicate the worms.

metas.name: Futurama - S3E5
metas.description: A double-date for Kif, Amy, Zapp, and Leela ends in disaster when their orbiting restaurant crashes on planet Amazonia. The hulking female inhabitants of the planet take their male captives to the omniscient Femputer, who orders Fry, Zapp, and Kif to death by "snu-snu".

metas.name: Futurama - S3E6
metas.description: Bender's urge to bend prompts Professor Farnsworth to send him on a rehabilitation visit to a steel factory, where he falls in love with a shapely fem-bot named Angleyne. But Bender's old rival Flexo and the intrusion of the Robot Mafia threaten to throw a wrench into the proceedings.

metas.name: Futurama - S3E7
metas.description: Earth is invaded by super-intelligent flying brains, who sap the Earth's populace of their intelligence. Leela is taken to Nibbler's home planet Eternia, where the Nibblonians explain that only one human is immune to the brains' powers - Fry.

metas.name: Futurama - S3E8
metas.description: Dr. Zoidberg reunites with his uncle, silent hologram star Harold Zoid, and the two of them set out to make a movie together. They cast the temperamental Calculon in the lead role, who demands an Oscar for his performance, but the movie doesn't go over well with audiences.

metas.name: Futurama - S3E9
metas.description: A sober Bender crashes a dark matter tanker on Pluto, threatening the penguin reserve nearby. Leela helps out in the clean-up, but when the penguins begin mating out of control, drastic action must be taken to thin the herd.

metas.name: Futurama - S3E10
metas.description: After a string of bad luck, Fry ventures into the decaying ruins of Old New York to regain his lucky seven-leaf clover from his childhood, only to find that his brother Yancy Fry had stolen not only the clover, but Fry's identity as well. Fry sets out to exhume his brother's body, but discovers the startling truth about Yancy instead.

metas.name: Futurama - S3E11
metas.description: Leela meets up with her former orphanarium playmate Adlai Atkins, now a plastic surgeon, who offers to grant Leela surgery that will give her two eyes. Meanwhile, Bender adopts twelve orphans in order to collect $1200 in government stipends.

metas.name: Futurama - S3E12
metas.description: Accused of robbing a bank, Fry and Bender plead insanity and are both sent to a robot insane asylum. While Bender and his buddy Roberto plan an escape, Fry is brainwashed into thinking that he is a robot.

metas.name: Futurama - S3E13
metas.description: When Bender is paralyzed in a tragic can opener accident, he discovers his musical washboard skills and goes on tour as a member of Beck's folk-rock band, acting as a voice for broken robots everywhere. Fry, Leela, Amy, and Zoidberg tag along in Fry's antique 1960s VW Van.

metas.name: Futurama - S3E14
metas.description: While creating a team of mutants to play the Harlem Globetrotters, the Professor accidentally causes a disruption in time that threatens the existence of the universe. Meanwhile, Fry tries to win an unreceptive Leela's heart.

metas.name: Futurama - S3E15
metas.description: Fry discovers the ability to download any celebrity onto a blank robot, and chooses to download Lucy Liu, with whom he falls madly in love. Repulsed by this disgusting display of human/robot love, Leela, Bender, and Zoidberg set out to shut down Nappster.com and put an end to illegal celebrity downloads forever.

metas.name: Futurama - S4E1
metas.description: A spacetime accident leaves the Planet Express crew stranded in Roswell, New Mexico in the year 1947, where a shattered Bender is mistaken for UFO debris and Zoidberg is captured as an alien specimen. Leela and the professor work to get everybody home, while Fry tries to keep his accident-prone grandfather alive.

metas.name: Futurama - S4E2
metas.description: A Planet Express mission to Robot Santa's colony on Neptune leaves the murderous robot trapped in the frozen sea, and Bender takes over as Santa, vowing to bring peace and goodwill to Xmas again. But when Bender is mistaken for the real Robot Santa, he is arrested and sentenced to death.

metas.name: Futurama - S4E3
metas.description: When the Professor fine tunes his "What-If" machine, Bender discovers what it would be like if he were human, Fry discovers what it would be like if life were more like a video game, and Leela discovers what it would be like if she found her true home.

metas.name: Futurama - S4E4
metas.description: As Valentine's Day approaches, Bender's romance with the Planet Express ship puts the crew in jeopardy.

metas.name: Futurama - S4E5
metas.description: Leela finally learns the truth about her parents and her own identity.

metas.name: Futurama - S4E6
metas.description: When Amy's parents' ranch is hit by a dust storm that blows away their herd of buggalo, Kif sets out to prove his masculinity by rounding up the herd, only to become entangled with the native Martians.

metas.name: Futurama - S4E7
metas.description: Bender fears that nobody will remember him after he dies, and sees his chance for immortality when the crew is enslaved on the planet Osiris 4. Posing as the planet's new pharaoh, Bender orders a humongous statue built in his honor, and quickly goes mad with power.

metas.name: Futurama - S4E8
metas.description: Bender is accidentally shot out of the ship's torpedo tube and becomes lost in space. Floating through the ethereal darkness, Bender becomes inhabited with tiny alien life forms, but has trouble playing God to their unyielding prayers.

metas.name: Futurama - S4E9
metas.description: With Planet Express in financial trouble, Fry nominates a flashy businessman from the 1980s to replace Professor Farnsworth as CEO of the company.

metas.name: Futurama - S4E10
metas.description: Leela endeavors to become the first female blernsball player, but her lack of depth perception hinders her skills. Nevertheless, she becomes the pitcher for the New New York Mets, purely for her novelty value.

metas.name: Futurama - S4E11
metas.description: Bender, insulted by the reception his terrible cooking receives, runs away and joins a space hobo camp, where he apprentices with a legendary former TV chef. Meanwhile, Dr. Zoidberg accidentally destroys Professor Farnsworth's prized ship-in-a-bottle and frames Fry.

metas.name: Futurama - S4E12
metas.description: The crew is pitted against the original Star Trek cast to win the devotion of an energy creature.

metas.name: Futurama - S5E1
metas.description: When Earth is jeopardized by global warming, the crew attends a conference in Kyoto hosted by Al Gore's head.

metas.name: Futurama - S5E2
metas.description: Bender grows jealous when Fry attempts to clone Seymour, his beloved fossilized dog.

metas.name: Futurama - S5E3
metas.description: Farnsworth's clone Cubert teams up with Hermes' son Dwight to launch a newspaper delivery business. Farnsworth and Hermes scoff at the kids' efforts - until the delivery boys accumulate enough capital to buy out Planet Express. Meanwhile, Fry and Leela use Bender to brew their own homemade beer.

metas.name: Futurama - S5E4
metas.description: Zoidberg becomes the center of a Supreme Court case after he celebrates Freedom Day by publicly eating the Earth flag.

metas.name: Futurama - S5E5
metas.description: When Kif gets pregnant, Amy questions her readiness to become a parent.

metas.name: Futurama - S5E6
metas.description: When Fry and Leela discover a cream that gives them unusual powers, they form a superhero team along with Bender.

metas.name: Futurama - S5E7
metas.description: The Planet Express crew is exposed to a substance that causes everyone to age backwards.

metas.name: Futurama - S5E8
metas.description: Nibbler enlists Fry in a mission to blow up evil brains bent on destroying the universe.

metas.name: Futurama - S5E9
metas.description: The Planet Express crew attempt the mission that killed the last crew: raiding a massive alien beehive for its honey.

metas.name: Futurama - S5E10
metas.description: The Planet Express crew goes to a parallel universe made accessible by the Professor's latest experiment.

metas.name: Futurama - S5E11
metas.description: With the help of a $300 government refund, the Planet Express crew pursue their dreams and desires.

metas.name: Futurama - S5E12
metas.description: Fry's nose is stolen by alien poachers to sell as a black market aphrodesiac.

metas.name: Futurama - S5E13
metas.description: Bender's scam to win Olympic gold sets him on the road to becoming a celebrity wife.

metas.name: Futurama - S5E14
metas.description: A new model of robot threatens Bender, forcing him to run away after he avoids upgrading his own technology.

metas.name: Futurama - S5E15
metas.description: Bender becomes a television star as well as a terrible role model for children.

metas.name: Futurama - S5E16
metas.description: Fry makes a deal with the Robot Devil so he can become a skillful musician and win Leela's heart.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E1
metas.description: After a devastating spaceship crash, Professor Farnsworth attempts to resuscitate the crew with his birth machine.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E2
metas.description: Leela and Zapp Brannigan find themselves stranded on an Eden-like planet.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E3
metas.description: The latest trend in social networking and having no life takes over Earth; and Fry posts an embarrassing video of Leela on-line.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E4
metas.description: Bender leads a campaign to make robosexual marriage between humans and robots legal on Earth.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E5
metas.description: The Planet Express crew races to future Rome to unearth the shocking secret of Leonardo da Vinci.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E6
metas.description: Bender learns that he suffers from a mortal manufacturing defect.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E7
metas.description: The Professor invents a one-way time machine that takes the crew ever further into the future with no hope of returning.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E8
metas.description: Earth is invaded by a race of intelligent cats.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E9
metas.description: The theory of evolution is put to the test on a planet inhabited by robots.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E10
metas.description: A revolutionary invention allows the crew members to exchange minds.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E11
metas.description: After a bungled Earth invasion, alien leader Lrrr faces a midlife crisis.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E12
metas.description: Leela leads an army of underground mutants in a rebellion against the surface people.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E13
metas.description: The Planet Express crew learns the true meaning of Xmas, Robanukah and Kwanzaa.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E14
metas.description: The crew members encounter a bizarre alien with the power to change their sexual characteristics.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E15
metas.description: Bender creates duplicates of himself, who in turn create duplicates of themselves, until they threaten to consume all of the matter on Earth.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E16
metas.description: Fed up with the crew, Bender decides to kill himself using a suicide booth. After he dies, his software takes on a ghostly existence.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E17
metas.description: Fry quits his job and becomes a police officer assigned to the Future Crimes Division.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E18
metas.description: After testifying against the Robot Mafia, Bender goes into hiding in the witness relocation program.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E19
metas.description: Leela becomes a Hollywood Big Shot after creating a hit children's television series.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E20
metas.description: The crew members alter history when they travel back in time to the American Revolution.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E21
metas.description: Leela becomes obsessed with hunting down a mysterious four-dimensional space whale.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E22
metas.description: Fry nurtures an alien egg that hatches into a horrific monster.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E23
metas.description: The crew uncovers a dark secret concerning a covert mission undertaken by the Professor and Dr. Zoidberg many years earlier.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E24
metas.description: Fry's sneezing reintroduces the common cold to the world of the future, with devastating consequences.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E25
metas.description: Bender evolves into a godlike being after vastly increasing his processing power.

metas.name: Futurama - S6E26
metas.description: Futurama is reconceived in three alternate animation styles: classic black-and-white, old-school videogame, and Japanese anime.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E1
metas.description: Bender fathers a child with the office soda machine.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E2
metas.description: An ancient prophecy predicts the world will end in the year 3012.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E3
metas.description: Leela becomes campaign manager for a presidential candidate whose birth certificate is mysteriously missing.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E4
metas.description: Bender joins the paparazzi and attempts to photograph a famous actor whose face has never been seen.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E5
metas.description: Zapp Brannigan loses interest in Leela and instead turns his attentions towards her mother, Turanga Munda.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E6
metas.description: Leela and Amy become addicted to the performance enhancing drug known as "Nectar" to increase their chances of winning at the epic sport of Butterfly Derby.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E7
metas.description: Hermes replaces parts of his body with robotic counterparts so as to increase his productivity.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E8
metas.description: Fry is the victim of a sausage-making accident during the crew's visit to Oktoberfest.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E9
metas.description: Bender searches for the meaning of life after learning that robots lack free will.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E10
metas.description: The Professor is deeply disturbed when his parents return from virtual retirement aboard the Near-Death Star.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E11
metas.description: The crew stages a casino heist to recover stolen property from the Robot Mafia.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E12
metas.description: After saving a robotic fox from hunters, Bender becomes the hunted.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E13
metas.description: Bender and the crew are reimagined as wild animals in a nature documentary.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E14
metas.description: Professor Farnsworth joins a gang of street-racing punks.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E15
metas.description: Fry and Leela's romantic vacation goes disturbingly wrong.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E16
metas.description: After being marooned on a distant planet, Fry is befriended by a young monster.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E17
metas.description: At a maximum security prison, Bender meets a famous folk singer and attempts to make a copy of his precious guitar.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E18
metas.description: Bender becomes a famous firefighter, but the crew suspects him of arson.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E19
metas.description: The Futurama gang stars in a trio of craptastic morning cartoons.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E20
metas.description: Bender goes grave robbing to bring his favorite actor back to life.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E21
metas.description: Bender searches the universe for his missing shiny metal rear end.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E22
metas.description: After a rare condition causes Leela to grow tentacles, she stumbles upon a secret genetic engineering facility.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E23
metas.description: The crew journeys into Fry's dreams to seek the meaning of a mysterious alien melody.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E24
metas.description: The crew becomes trapped aboard a ship with a horrific alien creature.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E25
metas.description: Dr. Zoidberg meets the love of his life while Bender squares off in a deadly tap dancing competition.

metas.name: Futurama - S7E26
metas.description: Fry asks Leela to marry him, and they face their destiny together as the Professor's latest invention alters the fabric of time.

metas.name: Futurama - S8E1
metas.description: Fry risks permanent insanity when he attempts to binge-watch every TV show ever made.

metas.name: Futurama - S8E2
metas.description: Amy and Kif’s children emerge from an alien swamp.

metas.name: Futurama - S8E3
metas.description: Bender and the crew head west to join the bitcoin mining rush.

metas.name: Futurama - S8E4
metas.description: After Nibbler falls ill, the crew shrinks down for a dangerous mission into a desert world contained within his litter box.

metas.name: Futurama - S8E5
metas.description: Bender uncovers the mysteries of the vast Momazon corporation.

metas.name: Futurama - S8E6
metas.description: Bender and Zoidberg travel through time to attack Robot Santa.

metas.name: Futurama - S8E7
metas.description: A pandemic ravages future Earth.

metas.name: Futurama - S8E8
metas.description: When Zapp Brannigan is canceled for crude behavior, Leela takes over as captain of the Nimbus on a critical mission.

metas.name: Futurama - S8E9
metas.description: The crew members are reborn as toys.

metas.name: Futurama - S8E10
metas.description: The crew investigates whether the universe is a simulation.

metas.name: Futurama - S9E1
metas.description: After Bender sells an NFT representing the "Concept of Bender", he feels lost and returns to his ancestral village in Mexico to rediscover himself.

metas.name: Futurama - S9E2
metas.description: Bizarre aliens force the crew to reenact all the classic children's games Fry played at his 8th birthday party... except this time, they are played TO THE DEATH!

metas.name: Futurama - S9E3
metas.description: A mysterious temp worker takes over Fry's job... as well as his entire life.

metas.name: Futurama - S9E4
metas.description: Bender becomes a matador in the sport of "Bug Fighting."

metas.name: Futurama - S9E5
metas.description: Leela becomes friends with a jealous chatbot.

metas.name: Futurama - S9E6
metas.description: The Professor's fast-fashion clothes are a smash hit, but an environmental disaster.

metas.name: Futurama - S9E7
metas.description: Hermes inherits a Jamaican coffee farm that holds the ruins of an ancient spaceship.

metas.name: Futurama - S9E8
metas.description: Amy sets out to collect the cutest toys in the universe – but they are not as harmless as they appear.

metas.name: Futurama - S9E9
metas.description: Futurama is reimagined in the form of classic children's mystery books.

metas.name: Futurama - S9E10
metas.description: When Fry proposes to Leela, he is overcome by a near-fatal attack of déjà vu.

metas.name: Futurama - S10E1
metas.description: Bender undergoes a risky treatment to make himself gigantic.

metas.name: Futurama - S10E2
metas.description: The crew ignites a volcano in a desperate attempt to slow global warming.

metas.name: Futurama - S10E3
metas.description: Fry is heartbroken to learn Leela's soulmate is someone other than him.

metas.name: Futurama - S10E4
metas.description: The crew journeys into a mysterious world inhabited by numbers.

metas.name: Futurama - S10E5
metas.description: Bender is sent off to Zapp Brannigan's camp for screen addicts.

metas.name: Futurama - S10E6
metas.description: Pandemonium erupts when people around the world start floating up into the sky.

metas.name: Futurama - S10E7
metas.description: A conspiracy theory makes shocking claims about the basement of the local pizzeria.

metas.name: Futurama - S10E8
metas.description: Leela's parents adopt Dr. Zoidberg.

metas.name: Futurama - S10E9
metas.description: Bender and his trusty pig set off to hunt space truffles in the truffle belt.

metas.name: Futurama - S10E10
metas.description: The crew embarks on an epic ten-million-year journey to witness the birth of a new universe.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S1E1
metas.description: Roisin Conaty, Tim Key, Romesh Ranganathan, Frank Skinner and Josh Widdicombe are tasked with trying to paint the best picture of a horse whilst riding a horse, plus there's melon-eating, bath-emptying and pop-up tents.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S1E2
metas.description: Taskmaster Greg Davies commands his captive band of comedians to identify the contents of a table full of mystery pies, make a film backwards and high-five a fifty five year old.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S1E3
metas.description: The comedians build a tower for an egg and make a video ‘blooper’. The results are kind of surprising and kind of scary. Plus, Josh Widdicombe gives Greg Davies the ultimate gift – one that they will both remember FOREVER.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S1E4
metas.description: This week, can Roisin Conaty, Tim Key, Romesh Ranganathan, Frank Skinner and Josh Widdicombe fill an eggcup with tears, remember an Aussie Rules football team or play Taskmaster squash?

metas.name: Taskmaster - S1E5
metas.description: Greg Davies and Alex Horne host as Frank Skinner takes a boulder onto a train for a pleasant excursion. Then, he and the other comedians try to make a Swedish man blush.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S1E6
metas.description: In the last episode of the series, the five comics are not only playing for homemade chowder but also for the inaugural Taskmaster series winner’s trophy. Tasks include playing golf with eggs and cooking a meal using ingredients starting with every letter of the alphabet.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S2E1
metas.description: Greg Davies returns as the Taskmaster as five new comedic competitors tackle a raft of tricky tasks with today’s episode including a potato-throwing moment set to make television and sporting history.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S2E2
metas.description: Nursery rhyme music videos are the flavour of episode two and viewer discretion is advised for Jon Richardson’s disturbing take on Three Blind Mice. Plus find out why Doc Brown isn’t an egg man.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S2E3
metas.description: Richard Osman and Doc Brown are amongst the funny folk who have to impress a guy in gold chains: the Mayor of Chesham! Greg Davies sets the nutty tasks.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S2E4
metas.description: Greg Davies draws on his wicked Inbetweeners alter-ego as he sets Katherine Ryan and other celebs cruel tasks, from pet rescue to pineapple concealment.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S2E5
metas.description: In the final episode Taskmaster Greg Davies finally gets to present his glorious trophy to the overall champion. But before that triumphant moment, there's still time for more ridiculous task attempts, including Richard Osman doing something appalling with a shopping trolley.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S3E1
metas.description: Greg Davies sets bewilderingly brilliant tasks - involving vegetable propulsion, snowmen and Swedes - to five celebs, including Al Murray and Dave Gorman.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S3E2
metas.description: Greg Davies thinks outside the box - or inside a cement-filled shoebox in Rob Beckett's case - as he sets nutty tasks. Dave Gorman gets all bitey!

metas.name: Taskmaster - S3E3
metas.description: Sadistic Mr Gilbert in the Inbetweeners, Greg Davies takes just as much pleasure from torturing celebs. Al Murray is confronted with an eggcup!

metas.name: Taskmaster - S3E4
metas.description: Greg Davies dishes out surreal tasks to celebs. The comics have to hand an item of clothing to a cyclist and grill an 83-year-old man called Hugh!

metas.name: Taskmaster - S3E5
metas.description: Exasperated comics are set absurd tasks! Before the series winner is crowned, we find out which celeb has the dishiest relative and why Rob Beckett is mad at peas.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S4E1
metas.description: Hugh Dennis, Joe Lycett, Lolly Adefope, Mel Giedroyc and Noel Fielding fell rubber ducks and destroy cakes to impress the very demanding Greg Davies.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S4E2
metas.description: Lolly Adefope tries long distance painting. Hugh Dennis is befuddled by an egg.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S4E3
metas.description: Devious Greg Davies tries to spot our five camouflaged comics, before the gang attempt to herd some dogs and chickens.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S4E4
metas.description: What will Mel Giedroyc do to a pommel horse? And why are Alex Horne and Noel Fielding dancing like shrews?

metas.name: Taskmaster - S4E5
metas.description: Noel Fielding has a sliding session, while Swedishman Fred receives an intense video call.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S4E6
metas.description: Joe Lycett shows no mercy to a camel. Hugh Dennis gets a sleeping bag challenge.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S4E7
metas.description: Hugh Dennis wields a blade, while Mel Giedroyc destroys a mannequin.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S4E8
metas.description: Noel Fielding finds his soulmate, while Mel Giedroyc bites off more than she can chew.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S5E1
metas.description: Greg Davies sets Aisling Bea, Bob Mortimer, Mark Watson, Nish Kumar and Sally Phillips brilliantly bewildering tasks, including basketball without hands!

metas.name: Taskmaster - S5E2
metas.description: Greg Davies takes great pleasure in celebrity humiliation. Nish Kumar pretends he's Robin Hood, while Mark Watson feels the wrath of yoghurt.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S5E3
metas.description: Proving he's as diabolical in real life as he is in the Inbetweeners, Greg Davies sets our five comedians absurd tasks such as jelly-balancing and coconut costumery.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S5E4
metas.description: Mark Watson and Nish Kumar pop toast together and Sally Phillips spreads absinthe on it. Greg Davies dishes out the absurd A-list challenges once more.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S5E5
metas.description: Greg Davies sets tasks that are bizarre, cruel - and also very funny. Sally Phillips faces an amorous watercooler. Aisling Bea crams a dish into a balloon.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S5E6
metas.description: The consensual celebrity humiliation continues at the hands of wicked Greg Davies. Bob Mortimer champions sausage meat, while Sally Phillips returns to the womb.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S5E7
metas.description: Evil Greg Davies sets five comedians cruel conundrums. Nish Kumar is presented with a coconut harness, while a blindfolded Sally Phillips handles a slice of bread.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S5E8
metas.description: The celebrity torture is almost over as the series climaxes. Aisling Bea leaps cushions, while Bob Mortimer educates us on Dumfries and Galloway.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S6E1
metas.description: Alice Levine, Asim Chaudhry, Liza Tarbuck, Russell Howard and Tim Vine tackle wheelbarrow stunts, lemon towers and a wardrobe as Greg Davies sets them truly absurd tasks.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S6E2
metas.description: Liza Tarbuck shows her hatred of small toy men. Asim Chaudhry invents a new rain hat. Alice Levine tries to fathom darts.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S6E3
metas.description: Liza Tarbuck introduces a plastic lizard to a drill.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S6E4
metas.description: Tim Vine strips to his waist, Russell Howard gets tattooed, while Alice Levine apes Leonardo da Vinci using squirty cream.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S6E5
metas.description: Asim Chaudhry has fun with drain pipes, Tim Vine gets stuck in a tunnel

metas.name: Taskmaster - S6E6
metas.description: It is snowy globes, rubbery ducks and wooden-y spoons, as the competitors use their ingenuity to impress Taskmaster Greg Davies.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S6E7
metas.description: Tim Vine encounters a wet bra, Alice Levine barely attempts cricket and Russell Howard makes a convincing liger.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S6E8
metas.description: Russell Howard charms a nice lady called Carol and Liza Tarbuck makes a puzzle for children, that should not be seen by children.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S6E9
metas.description: Russell Howard wraps a tie round his head and Tim Vine is left bereft by a small piece of bent metal.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S6E10
metas.description: Tim Vine attempts a risky impersonation and Alex Horne receives a gift from Liza Tarbuck. The recipient of the Taskmaster's golden bonce is revealed.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S7E1
metas.description: The Taskmaster Greg Davies returns, sending fear coursing through the veins of James Acaster, Jessica Knappett, Kerry Godliman, Phil Wang and Rhod Gilbert.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S7E2
metas.description: Phil Wang makes ogres poignant, Jessica Knappett mimics a funny noise and James Acaster finds circles. Alex Horne records it all for Taskmaster, Greg Davies.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S7E3
metas.description: Rhod Gilbert force-feeds mocha to Taskmaster Greg Davies' loyal assistant Alex Horne, Kerry Godliman boshes a ball and Phil Wang makes an odd noise.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S7E4
metas.description: Jessica Knappett piles shoes on a butcher's scales, James Acaster tries an emergency alarm and Rhod Gilbert smears mustard in his eyes - all to please Taskmaster Greg Davies.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S7E5
metas.description: Rhod Gilbert endangers Alex's life (again), Jessica Knappett's sunny outlook on life is severely tested by a former traffic warden and Phil Wang has a lovely old time with a squeezy jar of mayonnaise.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S7E6
metas.description: At the halfway stage, all contestants, with the possible exception of Phil Wang, still have high hopes of winning. Kerry Godliman and Jessica Knappett resort to soapy violence.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S7E7
metas.description: Greg Davies challenges his five fearful funny people to entertain him by completing more baffling challenges, which this week involve corneas, crackers and construction.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S7E8
metas.description: Phil Wang makes sure his outstanding outfit leaves even less to the imagination that it did before, Jessica Knappett demonstrates her mastery of a child's percussion instrument and James Acaster's dream comes true.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S7E9
metas.description: Two episodes remain in the series and with Greg Davies's shiny head trophy within touching distance, tensions are beginning to run high between the beleaguered contestants.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S7E10
metas.description: The series reaches its majestic climax. Who will win Greg Davies's coveted golden head to become just the seventh Taskmaster Champion in history?

metas.name: Taskmaster - S8E1
metas.description: Five new contestants - Iain Stirling, Joe Thomas, Lou Sanders, Paul Sinha and Sian Gibson - complete a series of tasks under the watchful eye of Greg Davies and Alex Horne.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S8E2
metas.description: The race continues for Greg Davies's golden head trophy. Today, an interesting selection of facial hair and a fascinating collection of continuous noises.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S8E3
metas.description: Joe Thomas struggles with the definition of a mammal, Iain Stirling devises an explosive chemistry experiment and Sian Gibson sends an NSFW text to Greg.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S8E4
metas.description: The competitive nature of Taskmaster bubbles alarmingly to the surface as a simple team task involving a hammock exposes tensions between two of the five comics vying to win.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S8E5
metas.description: Paul Sinha has some struggles with a basketball, Joe Thomas has a disastrous birthday and will Sian Gibson rescue Little Alex Horne from the sharp beak of an aquatic bird?

metas.name: Taskmaster - S8E6
metas.description: Alex Horne takes scrupulous notes as Paul Sinha squats in a telephone box, and Iain Stirling stakes his claim for the Scottish national football team.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S8E7
metas.description: Joe Thomas grapples grumpily with some awkward hand-wear, Sian Gibson seems to find proof that she may be psychic and Iain Stirling shows some tasty shortbread who's boss.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S8E8
metas.description: Beach balls, water and sand contribute to a seaside feel, a jolly tone that is interrupted when Greg Davies deals with the questionable actions of one contestant.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S8E9
metas.description: Paul Sinha, Lou Sanders and Iain Stirling become one while Greg Davies watches on, ready to issue his judgements. Sian Gibson seeks to redefine the definition of bounce.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S8E10
metas.description: Lou Sanders chews on some stationery, Sian Gibson is crushed by a caravan, Paul Sinha wrecks a camera, Joe Thomas gets cross and Iain Stirling waves a stick about.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S9E1
metas.description: Greg Davies is back, joined as ever by Alex Horne. The five celebs making grand entrances for series nine are David Baddiel, Ed Gamble, Jo Brand, Katy Wix and Rose Matafeo.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S9E2
metas.description: Ed Gamble gets reckless with a frying pan, Katy Wix asks for a pencil and Jo Brand shares a leisurely sandwich with David Baddiel.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S9E3
metas.description: David Baddiel steers some heavy machinery toward a representation of his childhood sweetheart, in the hope of impressing Taskmaster Greg Davies.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S9E4
metas.description: David Baddiel shows a readiness to bare his chest and Katy Wix robs a piggy bank. Meanwhile, Alex is powerless in the face of Ed Gamble's creative vision.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S9E5
metas.description: Jo Brand shows cowboys how to cowboy, Katy Wix tortures Alex Horne with the help of a garden arch and Rose Matafeo asks a stranger if they have a library card.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S9E6
metas.description: Jo Brand demonstrates an impressive grasp of street slang, Katy Wix uses a long part of her body to steal something and David Baddiel takes a sledgehammer to a problem.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S9E7
metas.description: The competitors are lured to a wasteland for a challenge involving eggs and medical equipment. David Baddiel spray-paints his face and Jo Brand reveals her psychic superpowers.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S9E8
metas.description: Greg Davies delivers angry appraisals from his comfy throne, as Ed Gamble dons a baby's bonnet, Katy Wix makes Alex Horne wince and Rose Matafeo does a spot of screaming.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S9E9
metas.description: The five comics go undercover as they try to impress Greg Davies with their attempts at high-level espionage.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S9E10
metas.description: It's the grand final, which features peas, teas and absent keys, as Ed Gamble, Jo Brand, Katy Wix and Rose Matafeo vie to go home with Greg Davies's massive face.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S10E1
metas.description: BAFTA-winning game show. Taskmaster Greg Davies and his sidekick Alex Horne test the wit of Daisy May Cooper, Johnny Vegas, Katherine Parkinson, Mawaan Rizwan and Richard Herring.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S10E2
metas.description: Johnny Vegas yells from a tall chair, Katherine Parkinson forgets how balloons work and Richard Herring flails around in a dinghy.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S10E3
metas.description: Mawaan Rizwan attempts a farm-related illusion in the hope of impressing domineering Taskmaster Greg Davies. Plus: pasta disasters and catapult calamities.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S10E4
metas.description: Daisy flirts with a security guard, Richard is surprised by some chickens and Johnny convinces Alex to wear sparkly shoes while operating heavy machinery.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S10E5
metas.description: The competing comics demonstrate their love of watermelon and Johnny tries to win points by grabbing a passing cyclist and introducing him to some Christmas-related equipment.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S10E6
metas.description: Daisy May Cooper gets rather cross, Mawaan Rizwan learns a lesson about waterfowl and Richard Herring wrings out a wet sock

metas.name: Taskmaster - S10E7
metas.description: Mawaan is not at all fazed when faced with a task involving office chairs, chickens and remote control cars, although the same cannot be said of his rivals. Daisy tries to decipher mysterious messages from Richard, and Katherine is baffled by a giant marble

metas.name: Taskmaster - S10E8
metas.description: Alex Horne gets an acting role, while Richard Herring delivers a star turn from behind a bush. Mawaan Rizwan nicks a cup of tea. Johnny Vegas takes a tumble.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S10E9
metas.description: The comics go all out to win in the semi-final: Richard Herring bleeds on beer mats, Mawaan Rizwan volleys oranges and Katherine Parkinson counts plastic balls.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S10E10
metas.description: Katherine Parkinson searches for a spider, Daisy May Cooper destroys a mannequin and Johnny Vegas's pocket watch finally comes in handy.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S11E1
metas.description: Charlotte Ritchie, Jamali Maddix, Lee Mack, Mike Wozniak and Sarah Kendall catch rats and have a smashing time delivering crockery on a scooter

metas.name: Taskmaster - S11E2
metas.description: The contestants argue about facial hair. Mike Wozniak vaults a high fence. Lee Mack struggles to see a balloon.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S11E3
metas.description: Lee attempts an audacious hammer throw, Sarah makes sitting down memorable and Jamali forgets his passport. As usual, Alex Horne offers the intrepid contenders moral support

metas.name: Taskmaster - S11E4
metas.description: Charlotte Ritchie climbs a ladder with some loo roll, Mike Wozniak tries out a new catchphrase, and Lee Mack terrifies an innocent member of the public

metas.name: Taskmaster - S11E5
metas.description: Greg Davies and Alex Horne set their captive band of comics humiliating missions involving cling film, fashion for insects, and face-based shape-shifting

metas.name: Taskmaster - S11E6
metas.description: Jamali decorates the caravan, Sarah is appalled by a kiwi fruit and, under severe pressure, Mike delivers a revelation so visceral that it leaves everyone in a state of horrified delirium.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S11E7
metas.description: There are fires, alarms, bizarre banquets and surprising sound effects, as our five comics complete pointless tasks under the watchful eye of Greg Davies and Little Alex Horne

metas.name: Taskmaster - S11E8
metas.description: Greg Davies is impressed by Charlotte Ritchie's snack-munching methods and Jamali Maddix's pretty wings, as the race to win Greg's shiny skull-trophy continues.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S11E9
metas.description: Lee has a makeover, Jamali gets destructive with some rubble, and Mike warns Greg's assistant Alex Horne about some potentially dangerous dairy produce.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S11E10
metas.description: Charlotte Ritchie stumbles into some sand, Lee Mack dunks Little Alex Horne and Sarah Kendall stacks fish fingers. Meanwhile, Jamali Maddix staggers around a bit and Mike Wozniak staggers absolutely everyone - again.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S12E1
metas.description: Greg Davies and Alex Horne welcome five fresh competitors, including Guz Khan, who are desperate to lay their mitts on Greg's golden head-trophy. This time: creepers, catapults and one (fake) cactus.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S12E2
metas.description: The contestants have a mountain to climb, but can they hack teamwork? Morgana Robinson inspects suspect doggy bags. Guz Khan slaps a space hopper.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S12E3
metas.description: Taskmaster Greg Davies struggles to contain his scorn as Desiree Burch does something inexplicable with sand, Victoria Coren Mitchell draws an exploding cat and Alan Davies hurts himself with a rubber band.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S12E4
metas.description: Alex Horne gets all fired up by a fiendish riddle, as crafty comics including Victoria Coren Mitchell and Morgana Robinson contend with peas, jam, sunglasses and songs

metas.name: Taskmaster - S12E5
metas.description: It's the 100th episode of Taskmaster. Greg Davies, Alex Horne and their merry band of comics celebrate the only way they know how: with tasks involving cling film and flapjacks!

metas.name: Taskmaster - S12E6
metas.description: Desiree Burch tackles tennis balls and gaffer tape, and Alan Davies finds out how many circles he can carry, as Greg Davies dispenses justice

metas.name: Taskmaster - S12E7
metas.description: Greg Davies attempts to make sense of Morgana Robinson sliding a pole through a sponge. Alex Horne lends his considerable acting skills to a promotional film.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S12E8
metas.description: Desiree Burch draws on her face, Guz Khan endures a ball to the groin and Alan Davies loses an eye, as the quest to win Greg Davies' golden head heats up among our comic contenders

metas.name: Taskmaster - S12E9
metas.description: Scores are tight in the penultimate episode of the series. Can Alex Horne give Desiree Burch goosebumps? Plus: paint throwing, and enforced yawning.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S12E10
metas.description: In the prestigious grand final, there's chocolate gobbling, bizarre body movements and indecent proposals, but which plucky competitor will be declared the Taskmaster series champion?

metas.name: Taskmaster - S13E1
metas.description: The Taskmaster drags Ardal O'Hanlon, Bridget Christie, Chris Ramsey, Judi Love and Sophie Duker into his lair so that he can pit them against each other and watch excitedly as they lose all dignity while fighting to win his golden-head trophy.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S13E2
metas.description: Ardal O'Hanlon makes sheep, Chris Ramsey gets younger, Judi Love becomes a queen, Sophie Duker wets teabags, and Bridget Christie gets lost in a small field.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S13E3
metas.description: Bridget Christie shoves a cuddly toy inside a traffic cone, Sophie Duker does a dinosaur impression, Ardal O'Hanlon is defeated by a robot, Chris Ramsey is impressed with a hose, and Judi Love is confident about everything except marbles.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S13E4
metas.description: Alex Horne suffers for his boss as he is pelted with milk and gin. Meanwhile, Bridget Christie has many questions concerning shoes, Judi Love is bewildered by bunting, and Ardal O'Hanlon looks a bit bereft.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S13E5
metas.description: Several members of the cast become baffled by things as apparently simple as a high five and a fish tank. Ardal O'Hanlon discovers that a mallet floats, Bridget Christie has a present for the Taskmaster, and Sophie Duker covers some keys in saliva.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S13E6
metas.description: Ardal O'Hanlon straddles a mannequin, Judi Love demands access to a puppy, Sophie Duker asks a very good question, and Chris Ramsey takes a sausage to the nostril. Meanwhile, Greg's tiny secretary Alex Horne shows off his mastery of mimicry.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S13E7
metas.description: A sound knowledge of food and drink is required as Ardal O'Hanlon indulges in a plant-based snack, and liquids are poured down a giant tube on Sophie Duker's head. Bridget Christie browns a bun, and Chris Ramsey refuses to consume dried spaghetti.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S13E8
metas.description: Greg Davies delivers more rough justice as Ardal O'Hanlon sings about leaning on a fence, Judi Love designs some classy headgear, Bridget Christie uses her phone, and Sophie Duker displays genuinely world-class levels of endurance and fortitude.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S13E9
metas.description: The race for glory heats up as we reach the business end of the series. Bridget Christie researches Welsh royalty, and Judi Love pokes a shoe under a curtain.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S13E10
metas.description: The furious race to claim Greg Davies' gleaming golden head trophy reaches its glorious climax in the grand final. Sophie Duker uses chopsticks to remove valve caps, and Chris Ramsey raps about aubergines.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S14E1
metas.description: Greg Davies and Little Alex Horne return to wreak havoc on the lives of comics Dara Ó Briain, Fern Brady, John Kearns, Munya Chawawa and Sarah Millican, who fumble frozen peas and shout about fish

metas.name: Taskmaster - S14E2
metas.description: Munya Chawawa falls in a bath, Sarah Millican searches for a door, and Alex Horne is as happy as a sandboy when he gets to supervise tasks involving admin, ducks and messy flour!

metas.name: Taskmaster - S14E3
metas.description: Can Fern Brady fly? Is Sarah Millican fireproof? And why would the notoriously brutal Taskmaster treat the gang to a tasty dessert? All these questions and more will be answered.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S14E4
metas.description: Dara Ó Briain displays a fabulous flair for folding, Fern Brady complains about being forced to tidy up, and Munya Chawawa does DIY magic with a coat hanger.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S14E5
metas.description: Sarah Millican gets her hands in some cottage pie, Fern Brady becomes Brandy Alexander, Munya Chawawa demonstrates a mastery of martial arts and John Kearns survives an operation.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S14E6
metas.description: Dara Ó Briain finds himself baffled by a quantity of milk, John Kearns does some elaborate skipping, Munya Chawawa does some elaborate jumping, and Fern Brady walks too slowly. Amid the carnage, Little Alex Horne finds himself largely ignored.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S14E7
metas.description: Sarah Millican flails about with a frying pan, Munya Chawawa pulls off a brilliant billiards shot, and all five comics spend far too long searching for a bath, given its size...

metas.name: Taskmaster - S14E8
metas.description: Fern Brady and Munya Chawawa are yet to claim a win. Can a rare tailless tiger bring them luck? Meanwhile, Dara Ó Briain gets frustrated, and Sarah Millican does something clever.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S14E9
metas.description: John Kearns blunts the business end of a sausage, Fern Brady struggles with a raspberry and Munya Chawawa unwittingly forces Sarah Millican into finding a novel use for a ping-pong ball.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S14E10
metas.description: Noble knights Dara Ó Briain, Fern Brady, John Kearns, Munya Chawawa and Sarah Millican battle to win this elaborate joust to become the 14th Taskmaster champion.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S15E1
metas.description: Greg Davies and Alex Horne return as Frankie Boyle, Ivo Graham, Jenny Eclair, Kiell Smith-Bynoe, and Mae Martin unravel some string, throw toilet brushes, and dance around a giant golden pineapple.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S15E2
metas.description: Alex Horne splits his band five ways for a task, which inspires Jenny Eclair to impart her wisdom about piles. Meanwhile, Frankie Boyle has strong opinions about golf.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S15E3
metas.description: Jenny Eclair has a lovely time sitting on a chair, new potatoes cascading onto her head. But Ivo Graham just thinks it's stupid. And who can keep it together for the all-important sausage quiz?

metas.name: Taskmaster - S15E4
metas.description: Kiell Smith-Bynoe finds himself playing bingo without any shoes on, Mae Martin plays an unusual but potentially brilliant drum solo and all the comedians create boats to carry eggs, in a race involving eggs in boats.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S15E5
metas.description: Taskmaster Greg puts the comedians' memory and musicality to the test. Frankie Boyle impresses with his falsetto. Kiell Smith-Bynoe wows everyone with his spoon retrieval abilities. And Mae Martin fakes their way into Alex Horne's wallet.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S15E6
metas.description: It's time to meet everyone's imaginary friends in a domestic and poignant setting. Greg Davies has some tricky judging to do when people question the definition of the colour gold, and nobody understands a simple egg-shelling task.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S15E7
metas.description: Greg and Alex take things up a notch by introducing a pulper to proceedings. Jelly babies cause friction between the teams. And we discover that Mae Martin and Ivo Graham's go-to noun is 'duck'.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S15E8
metas.description: The comics retrieve pineapples from a pond and inventively repurpose umbrellas. And in a fitting climax, Frankie Boyle goes to town on a gigantic cat scratching post.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S15E9
metas.description: Mae Martin immediately forgets the only rule to a task. Greg Davies demands to be educated about history, but the five comics only seem to know about the year 1066. And there's paint, stomping, poor time keeping and some impressively sneaky prizes.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S15E10
metas.description: For the comics final act, they contemplate using a hose, have an incredibly intense game of face-changing chess with Alex, and create something soothing for two very specific professions. Who'll reign supreme in this truly unparalleled gladiatorial arena?

metas.name: Taskmaster - S16E1
metas.description: Greg slaps Alex because of peas. Lucy Beaumont marvels at a canned blindfold. And everyone tries to put a big duck in a lake.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S16E2
metas.description: Julian Clary finds a former Taskmaster champion hiding behind a door, Susan Wokoma does something unexpected with mice and fish, and Sue Perkins demonstrates her love of portcullises.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S16E3
metas.description: Poor Alex Horne gets insulted by a panpipe-playing Sue Perkins. Meanwhile, Julian Clary destroys a table and Lucy Beaumont munches on a tree.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S16E4
metas.description: Assistant Alex finds himself having to choke down some vaguely cylindrical meals created by the five contestants. Greg Davies watches in awe, delight and sometimes disgust as Lucy Beaumont gets her flowers mixed up.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S16E5
metas.description: Susan Wokoma finds something hidden in a flower, Lucy Beaumont gets handy with loo roll, and Julian Clary makes some kitchen receptacles look a bit sinister. Meanwhile, Alex Horne gets intimidated by Sam Campbell.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S16E6
metas.description: Sue Perkins makes an extraordinary noise, Julian Clary gets on his hands and knees, and Lucy Beaumont creeps everyone out. Sam Campbell proves adept at identifying colourful birds, and Susan Wokoma is grateful for her formal acting training.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S16E7
metas.description: Lucy Beaumont wonders if she should risk flicking a forbidden switch. Julian Clary wonders if he needs more than one pie. Sue Perkins wonders if a deer head is preferable to a boundary line. And Sam Campbell rips off his own head.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S16E8
metas.description: Excitement reaches volcanic proportions as the mystery of the secret task is finally revealed to the baffled cast of comics. And who will get to wear the Special Hat?

metas.name: Taskmaster - S16E9
metas.description: Julian Clary makes smart use of a fishing rod, Sue Perkins reimagines Botticelli, Sam Campbell collects some coins and Lucy Beaumont asks Alex Horne to sit on a sled.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S16E10
metas.description: The series reaches a thrilling climax with Sue Perkins forgetting about the milk and Julian Clary performing a gentle assault. Who will lift Greg's golden head to the heavens?

metas.name: Taskmaster - S17E1
metas.description: Joanne McNally, John Robins, Nick Mohammed, Sophie Willan and Steve Pemberton are hoping they'll still have a career after tasks involving roller blinds, a floating primate and, as is traditional, eggs.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S17E2
metas.description: Nick Mohammed sheathes a water bottle with a sock, Sophie Willan attempts to take flight, and John Robins puts his faith in honey, as Taskmaster Greg Davies sits in judgement

metas.name: Taskmaster - S17E3
metas.description: Will Steve Pemberton's no-vowels gamble pay off? Why does Sophie Willan love to balance items on her head? What does Nick Mohammed think is going to happen to those eggs? And why is Joanne McNally sniffing Little Alex Horne's beard?

metas.name: Taskmaster - S17E4
metas.description: The contestants are forced to wrangle wild animals, with unpleasant results for Steve Pemberton and Sophie Willan. Plus: pickled onions, and a controversial cardboard tube.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S17E5
metas.description: Steve Pemberton experiments with G-force, Sophie Willan splatters a human pigeon and Joanne McNally rocks a traffic cone. In other news, Nick Mohammed gets trapped under a bed.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S17E6
metas.description: As the bonce-battle intensifies, Steve Pemberton delivers a timely message of peace. Meanwhile, Joanne McNally invents a catchphrase, and John Robins gets creative with beans.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S17E7
metas.description: Sophie Willan shouts the name of a body part in Alex Horne's flustered face. John Robins demonstrates the power of his forearms. And can Joanne McNally act out the word 'beer'?

metas.name: Taskmaster - S17E8
metas.description: Taskmaster Greg Davies has witnessed many bizarre moments, but this episode ups the ante - from oars for arms to a beatboxing wolf. And why won't Nick Mohammed stop winking?!

metas.name: Taskmaster - S17E9
metas.description: Alex Horne risks raising Greg Davies' hackles with a task in celebration of himself. Steve Pemberton has an expletive-laden row with a bin. Plus: a novel use for a cow's ear.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S17E10
metas.description: It's the grand final! But before a new champion gets the honour of clutching Greg Davies' gilded bonce to their proud chest, Nick Mohammed makes a magical prediction.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S18E1
metas.description: Andy Zaltzman, Babátúndé Aléshé, Emma Sidi, Jack Dee and Rosie Jones search for a seal, hurl yoga balls in a theme park and force-feed Little Alex Horne a bowlful of bubblegum.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S18E2
metas.description: Jack Dee has a dramatic wardrobe change, Rosie Jones presents her new baby, and Emma Sidi's got Tamzin Outhwaite on the brain. Plus: multi-headed beasts, and a very dull ghost.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S18E3
metas.description: Babátúndé Aléshé almost certainly knows what a rocket is, and Jack Dee's a dead ringer for a famous artist. Greg Davies awards points (some), praise (little) and pity (plenty).

metas.name: Taskmaster - S18E4
metas.description: Andy Zaltzman takes a carrot-based risk. Emma Sidi performs some cartwheel tricks. Rosie Jones menaces a member of the crew. Alex Horne rings a bell (or two!)

metas.name: Taskmaster - S18E5
metas.description: Greg Davies gags after opening a big jar, Andy Zaltzman makes a robot sing and a blond Jack Dee discusses hedgehogs.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S18E6
metas.description: Alex Horne shows off his prowess with pliers. Jack Dee adopts a charismatic New York accent. Emma Sidi enjoys a Taskmaster tipple. Babátúndé Aléshé makes a lovely little otter.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S18E7
metas.description: Rosie Jones feels badly let down by her comedy hero Jack Dee, while Babátúndé Aléshé shouts about a fortune cookie. And Andy Zaltzman predicts the end of the world.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S18E8
metas.description: Andy Zaltzman dirties Alex Horne's trousers, Emma Sidi reveals a startling belief about socks, Jack Dee does some energetic air cycling, and Babátúndé Aléshé phones a friend.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S18E9
metas.description: As the contest goes down to the wire, Jack Dee makes some tense phone calls and Rosie Jones smears brown stuff on a plate. Can Andy Zaltzman shoehorn cricket into another task?

metas.name: Taskmaster - S18E10
metas.description: Our five weary contestants compete one last time, in a grand final featuring tiny chairs, giant eggs and a honking Alex Horne. But who'll lift the legendary Taskmaster trophy?

metas.name: Taskmaster - S19E1
metas.description: Fatiha El-Ghorri, Jason Mantzoukas, Mathew Baynton, Rosie Ramsey and Stevie Martin kick-start the series with tasks involving vinegar, raisins and, as is traditional, peas

metas.name: Taskmaster - S19E2
metas.description: In the always-awkward first team task of the series, Stevie Martin invents a new way for humans to move, while Fatiha El-Ghorri sweeps the Taskmaster garden with a small broom

metas.name: Taskmaster - S19E3
metas.description: Fatiha El-Ghorri has concerns about aggressive waterfowl, while Rosie Ramsey goes flying, but it's Mathew Baynton crawling through a corridor that causes the largest commotion.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S19E4
metas.description: Greg Davies makes a regrettable promise involving his trousers. Alex Horne has trouser issues of his own thanks to Rosie Ramsey. Stevie Martin punches a mannequin in the groin.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S19E5
metas.description: Jason Mantzoukas learns that lollipop ladies exist. Fatiha El-Ghorri tells a disturbing story about a goat. Alex Horne is compelled to join in with Mathew Baynton's ode to DIY.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S19E6
metas.description: Poor Alex Horne is poked, chased and repeatedly dunked in the comics' pursuit of glory, while an unhinged Jason Mantzoukas causes havoc in the once-harmonious Taskmaster House.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S19E7
metas.description: Rosie Ramsey reveals a talent for trotting. Mathew Baynton spectacularly forfeits any remaining dignity, and in a not-unrelated event, Alex Horne's shoes are doused in yoghurt.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S19E8
metas.description: For the 12th time this series, Jason Mantzoukas chooses violence as the solution to a task. Fatiha El-Ghorri retches into a balloon. Stevie Martin relives a difficult memory.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S19E9
metas.description: It's the penultimate episode of the series, and Patatas the cat is back! Fatiha El-Ghorri knocks down an egg-shaped Alex Horne. And who's the master of multi-tasking?

metas.name: Taskmaster - S19E10
metas.description: A shiny-new champion is about to be crowned, but not before Fatiha El-Ghorri kicks in a door, Mathew Baynton threatens a puppy and Stevie Martin expertly burns some toast.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S20E1
metas.description: Greg Davies and Alex Horne return for a new series as Ania Magliano, Maisie Adam, Phil Ellis, Reece Shearsmith and Sanjeev Bhaskar compete to win Greg's affection and his head.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S20E2
metas.description: Sanjeev Bhaskar rotates around a hill of flour, Maisie Adam's mouth pokes through the Taskmaster's nose, and Little Alex Horne introduces Little Little Alex Horne to the world.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S20E3
metas.description: Ania Magliano can't tell snakes and worms apart, Maisie Adam relies heavily on the lyrics to Mambo No 5, and Reece Shearsmith gets irrationally angry in a game of chance.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S20E4
metas.description: Maisie Adam and Phil Ellis are repulsed by olives, while Reece Shearsmith's mystified by a voice note. And Alex Horne regrets giving Ania Magliano access to his phone...

metas.name: Taskmaster - S20E5
metas.description: Maisie Adam sees red, Phil Ellis strips to his base layer and Reece Shearsmith destroys a valuable treasure. The Taskmaster maintains order, with authority in good measure.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S20E6
metas.description: Ania Magliano lassos a weathervane. Sanjeev Bhaskar fails to crush an egg. Phil Ellis's sunny disposition costs him dear. Plus: a chopsticks ladder, and a new low.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S20E7
metas.description: Ania Magliano spews up some blue jelly, Sanjeev Bhaskar requests a comfort break and Maisie Adam squawks like an exotic bird, while Alex Horne gets to wear an important hat!

metas.name: Taskmaster - S20E8
metas.description: Alex Horne gets to oversee his favourite sort of task: one involving mystery, ducks and annoyed contestants. Reece Shearsmith balances on one leg for an impressively long time.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S20E9
metas.description: The scores are tight and the tasks are chaotic as Alex Horne dons his jockey silks, Phil Ellis and Ania Magliano endanger the crew, and Maisie Adam forgets the rules. Again.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S20E10
metas.description: The Final. As the closest-ever series of Taskmaster draws to an exhilarating finish, all five shipmates are still in with a genuine chance of bagging the trophy. But mutiny's in the air!

metas.name: Taskmaster - S21E1
metas.description: Taskmaster's back! Amy Gledhill, Armando Iannucci, Joanna Page, Joel Dommett and Kumail Nanjiani leave all dignity at the door as they hunt for worms and feed Greg his five-a-day.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S21E2
metas.description: In a particularly active episode, Armando Iannucci performs a daring stunt, Kumail Nanjiani moves bowling pins with oven gloves, and Joanna Page unwisely attempts a handstand.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S21E3
metas.description: Alex Horne treats the contestants to a song, but not everyone appreciates it. A grunting Kumail Nanjiani embraces Amy Gledhill and Joel Dommett while they quiz him about a horse.

metas.name: Taskmaster - S21E4
metas.description: Episode 4 of Taskmaster

metas.name: Taskmaster - S21E5
metas.description: Episode 5 of Taskmaster

metas.name: Taskmaster - S21E6
metas.description: Episode 6 of Taskmaster

metas.name: Taskmaster - S21E7
metas.description: Episode 7 of Taskmaster

metas.name: Taskmaster - S21E8
metas.description: Episode 8 of Taskmaster

metas.name: Taskmaster - S21E9
metas.description: Episode 9 of Taskmaster

metas.name: Taskmaster - S21E10
metas.description: Episode 10 of Taskmaster

metas.name: Peep Show - S1E1
metas.description: Mark and Jeremy find themselves in competition for the same woman: Toni from next door. This is not the entire problem with Mark and his love life, rather, he is also lusting after Sophie, a co-worker, but finds himself at a loss with how to put his feelings into action. Jeremy is also pre-occupied, but not with love, with his music.

metas.name: Peep Show - S1E2
metas.description: Jeremy faces a dilemma: money vs. art. Should he put all his time and effort into his music and try to 'make it' or should he be like Mark and get a 9 to 5 job to pay the bills (including three months of rent owed to Mark). Sophie is still the object of Mark's desires and he still fumbles about trying desperately to let her know how he feels.

metas.name: Peep Show - S1E3
metas.description: Mark and Jeremy go to a party to try and find some long lost love, or perhaps someone for the night. At the party Mark meets a cool goth 'teenager' named Valarie, who wants to take their acquaintance to the next level: bowling! This gives Jeremy a chance to hook up with Toni, and allows Mark to take his mind off of Sophie . . . or will it?

metas.name: Peep Show - S1E4
metas.description: Mark attends a work conference and in the process makes a new friend, Alan Johnson, which upsets the 'El Dude' partnership. This forces Jeremy to consider who could replace Mark as flatmate and best friend. Super-Hands is the potential replacement, but he doesn't match up as well. Mark grows a moustache, like his new hero, Johnson whilst Jeremy is haunted by flashbacks to 'the one bad thing' that happened when he was wrecked with Super-Hands.

metas.name: Peep Show - S1E5
metas.description: Jeremy tries to wow Toni with his new job in a music studio while Mark gets passed over for promotion in favour of Sophie, and goes slowly round the twist. They then attempt to solve each other's problems with the help of phone stalking and pepper spray.

metas.name: Peep Show - S1E6
metas.description: In the last episode of the series, death and terminal illness prove to be the keys to romantic success for Mark and Jeremy. Jeremy gets in with the Christians and Mark finds the perfect venue for a first date with Sophie - a funeral. Everyone's having a great time until the paramedics arrive.

metas.name: Peep Show - S2E1
metas.description: Can you love two people at the same time? Jeremy believes you can, and his gorgeous new American girlfriend puts his taboo bashing beliefs to the test. Mark believes you can only love one person. Besides, he and Sophie, the object of his unrequited love, are getting on really well, especially since he starting hacking into her emails.

metas.name: Peep Show - S2E2
metas.description: Jeremy is hired by an old school friend to make a soundtrack for a film he's making. Mark makes a new friend at work who turns out to hold a fair amount of prejudice. Meanwhile, Sophie spends time with Jeff.

metas.name: Peep Show - S2E3
metas.description: Jeremy wants to get closer to Nancy so he tries to impress her by joining in her volunteer work which involves working in a soup kitchen for the homeless.  Mark manages to get Johnson to think he is an alcoholic.

metas.name: Peep Show - S2E4
metas.description: Mark, determined to forget Sophie, makes an effort to go out with April - but manages to blur the line between enthusiastic new boyfriend and stalker. Jeremy joins a new band, Executioners Bong, but insists that he is neither a roadie nor the equivalent of Bez.

metas.name: Peep Show - S2E5
metas.description: Mark becomes depressed about Sophie living with Jeff, but Jeremy is not sure that self-mutilation is the answer. Besides, Jeremy and Jeff are getting on horribly well.

metas.name: Peep Show - S2E6
metas.description: Jeremy and Nancy are getting married, for visa reasons. Mark, determined to save his friend, will do whatever it takes to stop them...

metas.name: Peep Show - S3E1
metas.description: Mark has a run-in with some muggers, who steal his precious blackberry - and put him off his stroke with Sophie as a result... Meanwhile, Jeremy faces a tricky dilemma over the love of his life and a potential threesome.

metas.name: Peep Show - S3E2
metas.description: Mark and Sophie are finally beginning to go steady when Sophie is moved away from London for work. Mark is devastated, so Sophie offers phone sex as a solution, which Mark nervously accepts. Meanwhile, Jeremy is forced to consider getting an old friend sectioned.

metas.name: Peep Show - S3E3
metas.description: Jeremy plans to throw a secret magic mushroom party in the flat, where he hopes to seduce his ex, Big Suze. But when Mark suddenly comes home from work with gastric flu, Jeremy's plans go to pieces.

metas.name: Peep Show - S3E4
metas.description: Mark's plans for an enjoyable and productive week off work go out the window when his sister comes to stay. Jeremy clearly has the hots for her, and when Mark warns him to back off, Jeremy just wants her more...

metas.name: Peep Show - S3E5
metas.description: Jeremy gets called for jury service but soon complicates things by falling for the defendant. Elsewhere, Mark & Sophie go clubbing.

metas.name: Peep Show - S3E6
metas.description: Mark worries he and Sophie are drifting apart and proposes. Elsewhere Super-Hans decides to give up drugs, and Jeremy once again gets intimate with past love Big Suze.

metas.name: Peep Show - S4E1
metas.description: Mark continues to dread the impending doom of his wedding day, still questioning his actions from the previous season. Both Mark and Jeremy meet Sophie's parents and brother- leading to disaster with arson, sex with a parent and drunken arguments.

metas.name: Peep Show - S4E2
metas.description: Johnson returns, immediately taking a shine to Big Suze, proposing a deal involving her to Jeremy. Mark is caught up in Johnson's "Project Zeus" with his reputation at the workplace hanging in the balance. Geoff makes his first appearance of the season, and Sophie spends some alone time with Jeremy in the hotel room.

metas.name: Peep Show - S4E3
metas.description: In order to avoid spending time with Sophie, Mark gets a gym membership. Whilst there he discovers that Jeremy's ex-wife Nancy is working there. Mark tells Jeremy who then becomes desperate to get back together with her. He decides to take a job as a cleaner at the gym and sets about stopping her from dating Mark's personal trainer Matt.

metas.name: Peep Show - S4E4
metas.description: Jeremy meets his hero, techno musician Russell "The Orgazoid". He ends up working as his handyman, which leads Mark to become suspicious, as it seems that Jeremy's job consists of making smoothies for him. Meanwhile Mark meets Sally at his school reunion, one the few girls who actually ever liked him. It turns out that she has married one of Mark's childhood tormentors, Foz. Mark decides to try and seduce Sally whilst Sophie is away on a business trip.

metas.name: Peep Show - S4E5
metas.description: Jeremy takes Mark on a stag weekend, with a rented canal boat. There, they meet two sisters, Aurora and Lucy, and their father. Jeremy falls in love with Aurora, whilst Lucy becomes sexually attracted to Mark. The father offers Mark a job in India, a way out of his impending marriage to Sophie, whilst Jeremy accidentally kills, and then eats a bit of Aurora's dog.

metas.name: Peep Show - S4E6
metas.description: It's Mark's wedding day. He is torn about whether he should go through with it or not. Jeremy discourages him but Mark continues. On the way to the reception, Sophie breaks down and runs away. Jeremy comforts Mark as the 'El Dude Brothers' are reunited.

metas.name: Peep Show - S5E1
metas.description: It's not so long since Mark's new bride Sophie stumbled from the car, looking in appalled fascination at her husband of ten minutes and sobbed, "He's horrible!" As we return for the fifth series of this engagingly filthy comedy, Mark (David Mitchell) is getting drunk and maudlin on wedding champagne as his flatmate Jeremy (Robert Webb) urges him to go out on a double-date: "Beggars can't be choosers, she's an actual woman." Mark - remember, this is a man who once based his romantic strategy on the Siege of Stalingrad - arms himself with a copy of the Friends of the British Museum magazine and goes forth again to search for love.

metas.name: Peep Show - S5E2
metas.description: Mark and Sophie return to work, while Jeremy renews his acquaintance with the depraved Super Hans, in yet another filthy, funny episode.

metas.name: Peep Show - S5E3
